title: eryngium aquaticum var. ravenelii
intro: Eryngium aquaticum var. ravenelii, also known as the Blue Devil's Claw, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Eryngium genus and is commonly found in the southeastern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of Eryngium aquaticum var. ravenelii include its ability to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
